name: Portfolio Management — Multi-Asset Rebalancing
description: A diversified portfolio with structural tensions between asset classes, risk targets, and market conditions. The projector reveals correlated risks, concentration blind spots, and structural pillars of the allocation.
direction: Optimize risk-adjusted returns while maintaining liquidity and regulatory compliance in a rising rate environment

tensions.claim: Tech sector is 40% of equity allocation — highest returns last 3 years but concentration risk if sector rotates
Long duration bonds offer higher yield but rising rates cause mark-to-market losses that pressure total return
Private credit yields 300bp over public markets but locks capital for 3-5 years and is hard to value accurately
Options hedging protects downside but costs 1.5% annually which compounds into significant drag on long-term returns
Equity-bond correlation turned positive — the diversification benefit that justified the allocation no longer holds
Monthly rebalancing captures mean reversion but transaction costs and tax events reduce the net benefit to near zero
ESG constraints exclude 15% of the investable universe — improves narrative but reduces diversification and may hurt returns
International allocation provides diversification but unhedged currency risk adds 8% volatility — hedging costs eat the yield advantage
5% cash buffer for redemptions earns money market rates but opportunity cost is 400bp versus fully invested portfolio
Value and momentum factors are historically cheap but crowded positioning means the reversal when it comes will be violent
Implied volatility is low suggesting complacency but selling vol generates income that boosts Sharpe ratio in calm markets
Tracking error budget is 200bp — enough for active bets but performance is measured against benchmark, creating career risk for managers
Tail risk hedging is expensive in normal times but a 3-sigma event would draw down 25% and trigger forced selling
Moderate leverage (1.3x) improves capital efficiency but increases drawdown depth and introduces margin call risk in stress scenarios
